behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at module wiring: evaluate-stage command + artifact persistence API + artifact key mapping. A unit test of one helper could miss miswired artifact type at the call boundary. Rejected higher levels: no behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess (not System), no dependence on model output quality (not Agentic), and no multi-agent sequence (not Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage in-process with a real run-context object and test artifact store (or local DB fake used by production integration tests). Seed issue context, execute stage, assert one new `grounding` artifact exists and `plan` is unchanged/not written by evaluate.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module flow behavior (artifact read path + implement orchestration + artifact write path). Needs in-process wiring to catch read/write contract regressions. Rejected higher levels: text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; correctness here is about using available grounding and target artifact slot, not judging LLM output quality; single-stage flow, not multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Preload run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement flow with deterministic model stub, assert grounding is consumed in implement inputs and resulting output is written to `plan` while `grounding` remains intact.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is a local decision rule in deep-dive: when diagnosis exists, skip redundant `plan` write. This can be caught at a single function/object boundary by asserting no write call. Rejected higher levels: behavior text targets one conditional side effect, not cross-module handoff requirement; no real infra; no model-output dependence; no multi-step sequence requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test deep-dive decision function/handler with issue content containing diagnosis and a spy artifact writer. Assert writer is not called for `plan` and returned actions indicate no overwrite.

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: This is state handoff across local modules (run context persistence + artifact lookup + implement reader). Unit is too low because lineage bugs appear at module boundaries. Rejected higher levels: no requirement for real OS/network/subprocess; test can use deterministic artifact content without evaluating model judgment; sequence is producer->consumer handoff but not requiring full multi-agent quality validation.
behaviors.test_description: Execute evaluate-stage write into shared run context, then execute implement-stage reader in same in-process test harness. Assert implement resolves the exact grounding artifact lineage (same run id/context linkage) and receives that payload.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a full multi-step agent pipeline and checks downstream effect of earlier grounding on final plan. This is beyond single-step agentic validation and must verify sequential handoff across multiple agentic stages. Rejected lower levels: Integration would only validate plumbing with stubs; Agentic single-step would miss cross-step propagation/regression in end-to-end chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real chain end-to-end with fixed task input and real model calls across deep-dive, evaluate, and implement. Assert final `plan` artifact contains grounding-derived elements introduced during evaluate-stage (not just generic plan output), and lineage links show same run chain.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: Needs orchestration-level state behavior: missing optional artifact + fallback path + artifact-store integrity. Unit may miss corruption in repository/update wiring. Rejected higher levels: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency stated; correctness is fallback/control-flow and artifact integrity, not model output quality; single-stage fallback, not multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create run context with no `grounding` artifact and preexisting unrelated artifacts. Execute implement flow in-process; assert a valid `plan` artifact is produced, no exceptions occur, and unrelated artifacts are unchanged.
